prompt:
Address the gemini-code-assist review comment on flux PR #61.

The comment (id 3143509984): the new design uses 'Inter Tight' and 'JetBrains Mono' fonts (declared in style.css :root as --font-sans and --font-mono) but neither is imported or linked. The app currently falls back to system fonts.

Fix: link Google Fonts in index.html <head>. Use:

  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter+Tight:wght@400;500;600;700&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500&display=swap">

Pick the weights that the CSS actually uses — scan style.css for font-weight values and only include those (Inter Tight typically uses 400/500/600/700; JetBrains Mono usually 400/500). Don't pull weights you don't use.

If self-hosting fits the project's static-resources philosophy better, you could instead vendor the fonts under fonts/ and use @font-face rules. Use judgment — Google Fonts is simpler but adds a third-party dependency; self-hosting keeps the deploy purely static. Go with Google Fonts unless you see existing self-hosted assets indicating the project's preference.

After the fix:
1. Run the Playwright test suite ('nix develop -c npm test') and make sure all tests still pass.
2. Reply to the review comment using:
   gh api repos/gunk-dev/flux/pulls/comments/3143509984/replies -f body='<your reply>'
   The reply should briefly state what you did (linked Google Fonts / self-hosted) and confirm the fonts now load.
3. Push the fix to the existing PR branch.
